# solution.py
"""QuestHub / Team Collaboration Manager.

A self-contained, thread-safe, in-memory collaboration platform. Although the
product brief is framed around "QuestHub" (a role-playing game collaboration
tool), the domain model is implemented generically as a team collaboration
manager: users, projects, tasks, threaded messaging with attachments,
performance analytics, ratings, and exportable reports (CSV / PDF).

Design notes
------------
* Standard library only.
* A single shared re-entrant lock guards every mutation so the manager is safe
  to drive from many threads. Every sub-store exposes that same lock object.
* Identifiers are globally unique across every entity type and start at 1.
* All getters return deep copies so callers can never mutate internal state.
"""

import copy
import csv
import io
import threading
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enumerations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class UserRole(Enum):
    """Authorization role of a user."""

    MEMBER = "member"
    ADMIN = "admin"


class TaskStatus(Enum):
    """Lifecycle state of a task."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class ReportFormat(Enum):
    """Supported report export formats."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(Enum):
    """Kinds of reports the manager can produce."""

    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exception hierarchy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for every error raised by the manager."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when input data fails validation."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an action."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint would be violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with current state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain entities
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class User:
    id: int
    username: str
    email: str
    role: UserRole
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Project:
    id: int
    name: str
    start_date: datetime
    end_date: datetime
    owner_id: int
    description: str = ""


@dataclass
class Task:
    id: int
    project_id: int
    title: str
    status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
    assignee_id: Optional[int] = None
    deadline: Optional[datetime] = None
    started_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    completed_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Attachment:
    filename: str
    size: int
    uploaded_by: int
    uploaded_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Message:
    id: int
    author_id: int
    body: str
    project_id: Optional[int] = None
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    parent_id: Optional[int] = None
    attachments: List[Attachment] = field(default_factory=list)
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    edited_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Rating:
    id: int
    rater_id: int
    ratee_id: int
    score: int
    feedback: str = ""
    task_id: Optional[int] = None
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Internal store
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _Store:
    """A thin key/value container that shares the manager's lock.

    The shared lock makes every sub-store participate in the same critical
    section, which is what the concurrency contract requires.
    """

    def __init__(self, lock: "threading.RLock") -> None:
        self._lock = lock
        self._items: Dict[int, Any] = {}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Manager
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """Thread-safe, in-memory team collaboration manager."""

    def __init__(self, now: Optional[Callable[[], datetime]] = None) -> None:
        self._now: Callable[[], datetime] = now if now is not None else datetime.now
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._id_counter = 0

        # Sub-stores all share the single lock (concurrency contract).
        self.users = _Store(self._lock)
        self.projects = _Store(self._lock)
        self.tasks = _Store(self._lock)
        self.messages = _Store(self._lock)
        self.performance = _Store(self._lock)
        self.reports = _Store(self._lock)

        # Secondary indexes.
        self._username_index: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self._email_index: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self._tasks_by_project: Dict[int, set] = {}
        self._tasks_by_assignee: Dict[int, set] = {}
        self._messages_by_project: Dict[int, set] = {}
        self._messages_by_task: Dict[int, set] = {}
        self._ratings_by_ratee: Dict[int, List[int]] = {}

    # -- internal helpers --------------------------------------------------
    def _next_id(self) -> int:
        self._id_counter += 1
        return self._id_counter

    @staticmethod
    def _copy(obj: Any) -> Any:
        return copy.deepcopy(obj)

    def _require_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        user = self.users._items.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user

    def _require_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        project = self.projects._items.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project

    def _require_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        task = self.tasks._items.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task

    def _require_message(self, message_id: int) -> Message:
        message = self.messages._items.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message

    @staticmethod
    def _normalize_email(email: Any) -> Optional[str]:
        if not isinstance(email, str):
            return None
        value = email.strip()
        if not value or "@" not in value:
            return None
        local, _, domain = value.partition("@")
        if not local or not domain or "@" in domain:
            return None
        return value

    # ==================================================================
    # User management
    # ==================================================================
    def register_user(
        self, username: str, email: str, role: UserRole = UserRole.MEMBER
    ) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(username, str) or not username.strip():
                raise ValidationError("username is required")
            normalized_email = self._normalize_email(email)
            if normalized_email is None:
                raise ValidationError("a valid email is required")
            if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")

            name = username.strip()
            if name in self._username_index:
                raise DuplicateError("username already in use")
            if normalized_email in self._email_index:
                raise DuplicateError("email already in use")

            user_id = self._next_id()
            user = User(
                id=user_id,
                username=name,
                email=normalized_email,
                role=role,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.users._items[user_id] = user
            self._username_index[name] = user_id
            self._email_index[normalized_email] = user_id
            return self._copy(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            return self._copy(self._require_user(user_id))

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        with self._lock:
            users = sorted(self.users._items.values(), key=lambda u: u.id)
            return [self._copy(u) for u in users]

    def update_user(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        username: Optional[str] = None,
        email: Optional[str] = None,
        role: Optional[UserRole] = None,
    ) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)

            new_name = user.username
            new_email = user.email
            new_role = user.role

            if username is not None:
                if not isinstance(username, str) or not username.strip():
                    raise ValidationError("username is required")
                new_name = username.strip()
            if email is not None:
                normalized = self._normalize_email(email)
                if normalized is None:
                    raise ValidationError("a valid email is required")
                new_email = normalized
            if role is not None:
                if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                    raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
                new_role = role

            if new_name != user.username and new_name in self._username_index:
                raise DuplicateError("username already in use")
            if new_email != user.email and new_email in self._email_index:
                raise DuplicateError("email already in use")

            if new_name != user.username:
                del self._username_index[user.username]
                self._username_index[new_name] = user.id
                user.username = new_name
            if new_email != user.email:
                del self._email_index[user.email]
                self._email_index[new_email] = user.id
                user.email = new_email
            user.role = new_role
            return self._copy(user)

    def delete_user(self, user_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)

            for project in self.projects._items.values():
                if project.owner_id == user_id:
                    raise ConflictError("user owns a project and cannot be deleted")

            for task_id in self._tasks_by_assignee.get(user_id, set()):
                task = self.tasks._items.get(task_id)
                if task is not None and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    raise ConflictError("user has open tasks and cannot be deleted")

            del self.users._items[user_id]
            self._username_index.pop(user.username, None)
            self._email_index.pop(user.email, None)
            self._tasks_by_assignee.pop(user_id, None)

    # ==================================================================
    # Project management
    # ==================================================================
    def create_project(
        self,
        name: str,
        start_date: datetime,
        end_date: datetime,
        owner_id: int,
        description: str = "",
    ) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(name, str) or not name.strip():
                raise ValidationError("project name is required")
            self._require_user(owner_id)
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot be before start_date")

            project_id = self._next_id()
            project = Project(
                id=project_id,
                name=name.strip(),
                start_date=start_date,
                end_date=end_date,
                owner_id=owner_id,
                description=description or "",
            )
            self.projects._items[project_id] = project
            self._tasks_by_project[project_id] = set()
            self._messages_by_project[project_id] = set()
            return self._copy(project)

    def get_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            return self._copy(self._require_project(project_id))

    def list_projects(self) -> List[Project]:
        with self._lock:
            projects = sorted(self.projects._items.values(), key=lambda p: p.id)
            return [self._copy(p) for p in projects]

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        start_date: Optional[datetime] = None,
        end_date: Optional[datetime] = None,
        description: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)

            new_name = project.name
            new_start = project.start_date
            new_end = project.end_date
            new_desc = project.description

            if name is not None:
                if not isinstance(name, str) or not name.strip():
                    raise ValidationError("project name is required")
                new_name = name.strip()
            if start_date is not None:
                new_start = start_date
            if end_date is not None:
                new_end = end_date
            if description is not None:
                new_desc = description

            if new_end < new_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot be before start_date")

            project.name = new_name
            project.start_date = new_start
            project.end_date = new_end
            project.description = new_desc
            return self._copy(project)

    def delete_project(self, project_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)

            task_ids = list(self._tasks_by_project.get(project_id, set()))
            for task_id in task_ids:
                task = self.tasks._items.pop(task_id, None)
                if task is not None and task.assignee_id is not None:
                    assignee_tasks = self._tasks_by_assignee.get(task.assignee_id)
                    if assignee_tasks is not None:
                        assignee_tasks.discard(task_id)
                message_ids = list(self._messages_by_task.get(task_id, set()))
                for message_id in message_ids:
                    self.messages._items.pop(message_id, None)
                self._messages_by_task.pop(task_id, None)
            self._tasks_by_project.pop(project_id, None)

            message_ids = list(self._messages_by_project.get(project_id, set()))
            for message_id in message_ids:
                self.messages._items.pop(message_id, None)
            self._messages_by_project.pop(project_id, None)

            del self.projects._items[project_id]

    # ==================================================================
    # Task management
    # ==================================================================
    def create_task(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        title: str,
        assignee_id: Optional[int] = None,
        deadline: Optional[datetime] = None,
    ) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(title, str) or not title.strip():
                raise ValidationError("task title is required")
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._require_user(assignee_id)
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("deadline cannot be before project start")

            task_id = self._next_id()
            task = Task(
                id=task_id,
                project_id=project_id,
                title=title.strip(),
                status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                assignee_id=assignee_id,
                deadline=deadline,
                started_at=None,
                completed_at=None,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.tasks._items[task_id] = task
            self._tasks_by_project.setdefault(project_id, set()).add(task_id)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._tasks_by_assignee.setdefault(assignee_id, set()).add(task_id)
            return self._copy(task)

    def get_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            return self._copy(self._require_task(task_id))

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            task_ids = self._tasks_by_project.get(project_id, set())
            tasks = sorted(
                (self.tasks._items[t] for t in task_ids), key=lambda t: t.id
            )
            return [self._copy(t) for t in tasks]

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            task_ids = self._tasks_by_assignee.get(user_id, set())
            tasks = sorted(
                (self.tasks._items[t] for t in task_ids), key=lambda t: t.id
            )
            return [self._copy(t) for t in tasks]

    def assign_task(self, task_id: int, assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            self._move_assignment(task, assignee_id)
            return self._copy(task)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id: int, new_assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(new_assignee_id)
            self._move_assignment(task, new_assignee_id)
            return self._copy(task)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._move_assignment(task, None)
            return self._copy(task)

    def _move_assignment(self, task: Task, assignee_id: Optional[int]) -> None:
        old = task.assignee_id
        if old is not None:
            current = self._tasks_by_assignee.get(old)
            if current is not None:
                current.discard(task.id)
        task.assignee_id = assignee_id
        if assignee_id is not None:
            self._tasks_by_assignee.setdefault(assignee_id, set()).add(task.id)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id: int, deadline: Optional[datetime]) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self.projects._items.get(task.project_id)
                if project is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError("deadline cannot be before project start")
            task.deadline = deadline
            return self._copy(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id: int, status: TaskStatus) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")

            now = self._now()
            if status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED:
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now
            task.status = status
            return self._copy(task)

    # ==================================================================
    # Messaging
    # ==================================================================
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id: int,
        body: str,
        project_id: Optional[int] = None,
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
        parent_id: Optional[int] = None,
        attachments: Optional[List[Tuple[str, bytes]]] = None,
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(author_id)
            if project_id is None and task_id is None:
                raise ValidationError("a project_id or task_id is required")
            if project_id is not None and task_id is not None:
                raise ValidationError("only one of project_id or task_id is allowed")
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)
            if parent_id is not None:
                self._require_message(parent_id)

            attachment_specs = attachments or []
            body_text = body if isinstance(body, str) else ""
            if not body_text.strip() and not attachment_specs:
                raise ValidationError("message body or an attachment is required")

            now = self._now()
            attachment_objs = [
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=len(data),
                    uploaded_by=author_id,
                    uploaded_at=now,
                )
                for filename, data in attachment_specs
            ]

            message_id = self._next_id()
            message = Message(
                id=message_id,
                author_id=author_id,
                body=body_text,
                project_id=project_id,
                task_id=task_id,
                parent_id=parent_id,
                attachments=attachment_objs,
                created_at=now,
                edited_at=None,
            )
            self.messages._items[message_id] = message
            if project_id is not None:
                self._messages_by_project.setdefault(project_id, set()).add(message_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._messages_by_task.setdefault(task_id, set()).add(message_id)
            return self._copy(message)

    def edit_message(self, message_id: int, editor_id: int, new_body: str) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may edit this message")
            body_text = new_body if isinstance(new_body, str) else ""
            if not body_text.strip() and not message.attachments:
                raise ValidationError("message body or an attachment is required")
            message.body = body_text
            message.edited_at = self._now()
            return self._copy(message)

    def add_attachment(
        self, message_id: int, uploader_id: int, filename: str, data: bytes
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != uploader_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may add attachments")
            if not isinstance(filename, str) or not filename.strip():
                raise ValidationError("filename is required")
            message.attachments.append(
                Attachment(
                    filename=filename,
                    size=len(data),
                    uploaded_by=uploader_id,
                    uploaded_at=self._now(),
                )
            )
            return self._copy(message)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            message_ids = self._messages_by_project.get(project_id, set())
            messages = sorted(
                (self.messages._items[m] for m in message_ids), key=lambda m: m.id
            )
            return [self._copy(m) for m in messages]

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_task(task_id)
            message_ids = self._messages_by_task.get(task_id, set())
            messages = sorted(
                (self.messages._items[m] for m in message_ids), key=lambda m: m.id
            )
            return [self._copy(m) for m in messages]

    def delete_message(self, message_id: int, requester_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != requester_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may delete this message")
            del self.messages._items[message_id]
            if message.project_id is not None:
                group = self._messages_by_project.get(message.project_id)
                if group is not None:
                    group.discard(message_id)
            if message.task_id is not None:
                group = self._messages_by_task.get(message.task_id)
                if group is not None:
                    group.discard(message_id)

    # ==================================================================
    # Performance dashboard
    # ==================================================================
    def _assigned_tasks(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        return [
            self.tasks._items[t]
            for t in self._tasks_by_assignee.get(user_id, set())
            if t in self.tasks._items
        ]

    @staticmethod
    def _completion_rate(tasks: List[Task]) -> float:
        if not tasks:
            return 0.0
        completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
        return completed / len(tasks)

    @staticmethod
    def _average_completion_time(tasks: List[Task]) -> Optional[float]:
        durations = [
            (t.completed_at - t.started_at).total_seconds()
            for t in tasks
            if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            and t.started_at is not None
            and t.completed_at is not None
        ]
        if not durations:
            return None
        return sum(durations) / len(durations)

    def _rating_stats(self, user_id: int) -> Tuple[int, float]:
        rating_ids = self._ratings_by_ratee.get(user_id, [])
        ratings = [self.performance._items[r] for r in rating_ids]
        count = len(ratings)
        average = sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count else 0.0
        return count, average

    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id: int) -> float:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            return self._completion_rate(self._assigned_tasks(user_id))

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id: int) -> Optional[float]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            return self._average_completion_time(self._assigned_tasks(user_id))

    def submit_rating(
        self,
        rater_id: int,
        ratee_id: int,
        score: int,
        feedback: str = "",
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> Rating:
        with self._lock:
            if isinstance(score, bool) or not isinstance(score, int):
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer from 1 to 5")
            if score < 1 or score > 5:
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer from 1 to 5")
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("a user cannot rate themselves")
            self._require_user(rater_id)
            self._require_user(ratee_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)

            rating_id = self._next_id()
            rating = Rating(
                id=rating_id,
                rater_id=rater_id,
                ratee_id=ratee_id,
                score=score,
                feedback=feedback or "",
                task_id=task_id,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.performance._items[rating_id] = rating
            self._ratings_by_ratee.setdefault(ratee_id, []).append(rating_id)
            return self._copy(rating)

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            rating_ids = self._ratings_by_ratee.get(user_id, [])
            ratings = [self.performance._items[r] for r in rating_ids]
            count = len(ratings)
            average = sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count else 0.0
            return {
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": [self._copy(r) for r in ratings],
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._assigned_tasks(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            in_progress = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
            not_started = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED)
            rating_count, rating_average = self._rating_stats(user_id)
            return {
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": self._completion_rate(tasks),
                "average_completion_time": self._average_completion_time(tasks),
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
            }

    # ==================================================================
    # Reports
    # ==================================================================
    def project_progress_report(self, project_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            task_ids = self._tasks_by_project.get(project_id, set())
            tasks = [self.tasks._items[t] for t in task_ids]

            now = self._now()
            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            completed = 0
            overdue = 0
            per_assignee: Dict[int, Dict[str, int]] = {}

            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    completed += 1
                if (
                    task.deadline is not None
                    and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                    and task.deadline < now
                ):
                    overdue += 1
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    stats = per_assignee.setdefault(
                        task.assignee_id, {"total": 0, "completed": 0}
                    )
                    stats["total"] += 1
                    if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                        stats["completed"] += 1

            total = len(tasks)
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
            }

    def _performance_row(self, user_id: int, tasks: List[Task]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        total = len(tasks)
        completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
        in_progress = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
        not_started = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED)
        rating_count, rating_average = self._rating_stats(user_id)
        return {
            "user_id": user_id,
            "total_tasks": total,
            "completed_tasks": completed,
            "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
            "not_started_tasks": not_started,
            "completion_rate": self._completion_rate(tasks),
            "average_completion_time": self._average_completion_time(tasks),
            "rating_count": rating_count,
            "rating_average": rating_average,
        }

    def team_performance_report(
        self, project_id: Optional[int] = None
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
                scope_tasks = [
                    self.tasks._items[t]
                    for t in self._tasks_by_project.get(project_id, set())
                ]
            else:
                scope_tasks = list(self.tasks._items.values())

            ordered_user_ids: List[int] = []
            seen = set()
            for task in scope_tasks:
                if task.assignee_id is not None and task.assignee_id not in seen:
                    seen.add(task.assignee_id)
                    ordered_user_ids.append(task.assignee_id)
            ordered_user_ids.sort()

            rows = []
            for user_id in ordered_user_ids:
                user_tasks = [t for t in scope_tasks if t.assignee_id == user_id]
                rows.append(self._performance_row(user_id, user_tasks))

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = sorted(self._assigned_tasks(user_id), key=lambda t: t.id)
            row = self._performance_row(user_id, tasks)
            row["report_type"] = ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value
            row["tasks"] = [self._copy(t) for t in tasks]
            return row

    def export_report(self, report: Dict[str, Any], fmt: ReportFormat) -> Any:
        if not isinstance(report, dict):
            raise ValidationError("report must be a report dictionary")
        if not isinstance(fmt, ReportFormat):
            raise ValidationError("fmt must be a ReportFormat")
        if fmt == ReportFormat.CSV:
            return self._report_to_csv(report)
        return self._report_to_pdf(report)

    # -- export helpers ----------------------------------------------------
    @staticmethod
    def _report_to_csv(report: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
        output = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(output)
        report_type = report.get("report_type")

        perf_columns = [
            "user_id",
            "total_tasks",
            "completed_tasks",
            "in_progress_tasks",
            "not_started_tasks",
            "completion_rate",
            "average_completion_time",
            "rating_count",
            "rating_average",
        ]

        if report_type == ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value:
            writer.writerow(perf_columns)
            for row in report.get("rows", []):
                writer.writerow([row.get(col, "") for col in perf_columns])
        elif report_type == ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value:
            writer.writerow(perf_columns)
            writer.writerow([report.get(col, "") for col in perf_columns])
        elif report_type == ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value:
            writer.writerow(["metric", "value"])
            writer.writerow(["project_id", report.get("project_id", "")])
            writer.writerow(["total_tasks", report.get("total_tasks", 0)])
            writer.writerow(["completion_rate", report.get("completion_rate", 0.0)])
            writer.writerow(["overdue_tasks", report.get("overdue_tasks", 0)])
            for status, count in report.get("status_counts", {}).items():
                writer.writerow(["status:" + str(status), count])
            for assignee_id, stats in report.get("per_assignee", {}).items():
                writer.writerow(
                    [
                        "assignee:" + str(assignee_id),
                        "{0}/{1}".format(
                            stats.get("completed", 0), stats.get("total", 0)
                        ),
                    ]
                )
        else:
            writer.writerow(["key", "value"])
            for key, value in report.items():
                writer.writerow([key, value])

        return output.getvalue()

    def _report_to_pdf(self, report: Dict[str, Any]) -> bytes:
        lines = self._report_to_lines(report)
        return self._build_pdf(lines)

    @staticmethod
    def _report_to_lines(report: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[str]:
        lines: List[str] = []
        report_type = report.get("report_type", "report")
        lines.append("Report: {0}".format(report_type))
        for key, value in report.items():
            if key == "report_type":
                continue
            if key == "rows" and isinstance(value, list):
                lines.append("rows:")
                for row in value:
                    lines.append("  " + ", ".join(
                        "{0}={1}".format(k, v) for k, v in row.items()
                    ))
            elif key == "tasks" and isinstance(value, list):
                lines.append("tasks: {0}".format(len(value)))
            elif isinstance(value, dict):
                lines.append("{0}:".format(key))
                for sub_key, sub_value in value.items():
                    lines.append("  {0}: {1}".format(sub_key, sub_value))
            else:
                lines.append("{0}: {1}".format(key, value))
        return lines or ["Empty report"]

    @staticmethod
    def _build_pdf(lines: List[str]) -> bytes:
        def escape(text: str) -> str:
            return (
                text.replace("\\", "\\\\")
                .replace("(", "\\(")
                .replace(")", "\\)")
            )

        content_parts = ["BT", "/F1 12 Tf", "50 750 Td", "14 TL"]
        for index, line in enumerate(lines):
            safe = escape(str(line))
            if index == 0:
                content_parts.append("({0}) Tj".format(safe))
            else:
                content_parts.append("T*")
                content_parts.append("({0}) Tj".format(safe))
        content_parts.append("ET")
        content = "\n".join(content_parts)
        content_bytes = content.encode("latin-1", "replace")

        objects = [
            b"<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
            b"<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
            (
                b"<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
                b"/Contents 4 0 R /Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> >>"
            ),
            b"<< /Length "
            + str(len(content_bytes)).encode("ascii")
            + b" >>\nstream\n"
            + content_bytes
            + b"\nendstream",
            b"<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
        ]

        pdf = bytearray(b"%PDF-1.4\n")
        offsets = []
        for number, body in enumerate(objects, start=1):
            offsets.append(len(pdf))
            pdf += str(number).encode("ascii") + b" 0 obj\n" + body + b"\nendobj\n"

        xref_offset = len(pdf)
        size = len(objects) + 1
        pdf += b"xref\n"
        pdf += b"0 " + str(size).encode("ascii") + b"\n"
        pdf += b"0000000000 65535 f \n"
        for offset in offsets:
            pdf += "{0:010d} 00000 n \n".format(offset).encode("ascii")
        pdf += b"trailer\n"
        pdf += b"<< /Size " + str(size).encode("ascii") + b" /Root 1 0 R >>\n"
        pdf += b"startxref\n"
        pdf += str(xref_offset).encode("ascii") + b"\n"
        pdf += b"%%EOF\n"
        return bytes(pdf)


__all__ = [
    "TeamCollaborationManager",
    "TaskStatus",
    "UserRole",
    "ReportFormat",
    "ReportType",
    "TeamCollaborationError",
    "ValidationError",
    "NotFoundError",
    "AuthorizationError",
    "DuplicateError",

]
